concerns.text: FR-006 requires the system to 'capture and process actual VR interaction data' (response times, gaze tracking, in-VR judgment inputs). The plan (Section 3.1, 3.2, and Research.md) explicitly states that 'Actual VR interaction logs are not available' and that the system will 'simulate' these logs and the 'Moral Stories' dataset. The plan does not include a task to capture real data or a mechanism to integrate it when available; it relies entirely on synthetic generation. This leaves the core requirement of processing 'actual' data unbacked.

FR-001 requires ingesting the 'Moral Stories' dataset from HuggingFace. The plan (Research.md, Section 2) acknowledges 'No verified source in block' for this dataset and explicitly decides to 'simulate' the data structure or use hardcoded vignettes instead of ingesting the specified dataset. The plan lacks a task to ingest the actual 'Moral Stories' dataset, leaving FR-001 partially unbacked.

SC-003 requires measuring interaction significance via 'Bonferroni-corrected p-value'. While the plan mentions running a mixed-effects model (US-3), it relies on synthetic data for the interaction terms. Without actual VR interaction data (as noted in FR-006 gap), the statistical significance of the interaction between 'salience' and 'foundation scores' cannot be empirically measured against real-world variance, rendering the success criterion untestable in the proposed plan.
